## Operational Notes

### Building the Union Population
```
Final Asthma Population =
  UNION of Patient.id from:
    Query 1  (Condition — problem list diagnosis)
  + Query 2  (MedicationRequest — albuterol / levalbuterol)
  + Query 3  (MedicationRequest — fluticasone products)
  + Query 4  (MedicationRequest — budesonide products)
  + Query 5  (MedicationRequest — montelukast)
  → de-duplicate on Patient.id
```

### Interpreting Each Patient Segment

| Segment | Patients Found | Recommended Action |
|---|---|---|
| Query 1 only | Diagnosed asthma, not on active med | Review — may be well-controlled or lost to follow-up |
| Queries 2–5 only | On asthma meds, **no formal diagnosis** | Priority for outreach — add asthma to problem list if clinically appropriate |
| Query 1 + any of 2–5 | Diagnosed and actively treated | Standard disease management enrollment |
| Queries 2–5 only, albuterol only | SABA monotherapy | Clinical review — may need step-up to controller therapy per GINA guidelines |

---

### Important Caveats & Recommended Adjustments

#### 1. Capture Historical Prescriptions (Broader Net)
The queries above use `status=active`. To catch patients whose prescriptions were recently completed or stopped — who may still have active asthma — expand the status filter:

```
MedicationRequest?code=...&status=active,on-hold,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

#### 2. If Your Server Supports Subsumption (`code:below`)
For SNOMED CT hierarchical search (if your FHIR server supports terminology subsumption), you can collapse Query 1 to a single parent code and let the server traverse child concepts:

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|195967001&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&_include=Condition:patient
```

#### 3. Add Encounter Diagnoses (Catches Consult Notes)
Your Query 1 targets `problem-list-item` only. To also catch asthma coded as an encounter/visit diagnosis (e.g., a pulmonology consult note that never pushed to the problem list), run a parallel condition query without the category filter:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|195967001,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J45.20,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J45.30,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J45.40,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J45.50,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J45.909&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|encounter-diagnosis&_include=Condition:patient
```

#### 4. Montelukast — Non-Asthma Overlap
Montelukast (Query 5) is also prescribed for **allergic rhinitis**. Patients returned by Query 5 with no asthma diagnosis and no SABA/ICS prescription should be reviewed manually before program enrollment to exclude rhinitis-only prescribing.

#### 5. Pagination
For large patient panels, add `_count` and iterate using the `next` link in Bundle responses:

```
MedicationRequest?code=...&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_count=200
```

#### 6. Consider Adding `MedicationDispense` for Fill Confirmation
A `MedicationRequest` confirms prescribing intent but not actual dispensing. If your system captures pharmacy dispense data, a parallel query can confirm the patient actually filled the prescription:

```
MedicationDispense?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|435,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|745679&whenhandedover=ge2025-05-22&_include=MedicationDispense:patient
```

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|435,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41126,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28779,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|831346

|---|---|---|

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
